items.title: The 'quiet details' shared by two artists working 50 years apart

children.content: The new exhibition at the National Portrait Gallery brings together the work of two Australian artists working nearly half a century apart, and asks visitors to find the sparks connecting them.

gallery.altText: An artwork of two women from the shoulders up.
A painting of a cat looking in the mirror hung above a bathroom sink.
An oil painting of dancers in a competition.

thumbnail.altText: An artwork of two women from the shoulders up.
A painting of a cat looking in the mirror hung above a bathroom sink.
An oil painting of dancers in a competition.

items.title: Too young for social media, but old enough to be locked up

children.content: As a nation, we have decided that a teenager cannot navigate a social media algorithm, but a 10-year-old can navigate a courtroom. For Australia's 2025 UN youth representative Satara Uthayakumaran, the contradiction is impossible to ignore.

image.altText: A young women sits listening to young unidentified detainees in a youth detention centre

items.title: Motorcyclist dies in crash in southern NSW

children.content: The state has reported its first road death of the Easter long weekend, with the detour expected to add five hours' travel time for some motorists.

image.altText: Generic sign of the Monaro Highway at Nimmitabel.

items.title: Thousands flock to National Folk Festival despite fuel cost concerns

children.content: The lure of a lively celebration of folk art outweighs concerns about the cost of fuel for thousands travelling to Canberra for the 60th National Folk Festival.

gallery.altText: A man dressed in an elaborate costume.

thumbnail.altText: A man dressed in an elaborate costume.

items.title: Raiders' season lurches towards disaster as Knights run riot

children.content: The reigning minor premiers are in freefall after Canberra slumped to a fourth-straight loss at the hands of a resurgent Knights outfit.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in orange celebrate while players in green watch on

items.title: 'Cameras watching': Horrifying trend of technology-based abuse

children.content: One in three women on temporary visas are experiencing domestic and family violence, safety advocates say.

image.altText: A professional with glasses and a sombre expression.

items.title: These 'readers' created in remote NT are unlike those in most schools

children.content: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

gallery.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

thumbnail.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

gallery.altText: A imagery with graphics effect showing a young girl in a pink sweater. Behind is a focus on an undentified craft over an oval

thumbnail.altText: A imagery with graphics effect showing a young girl in a pink sweater. Behind is a focus on an undentified craft over an oval

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

image.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

gallery.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

thumbnail.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: Search for 65yo swept away crossing floodwater on foot

children.content: Police are searching for a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A man looks into the camera. He has white hair and is wearing a hat.

items.title: Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel

children.content: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

image.altText: A large cross and a flag of Lebanon erected on top of hill, underneath large letters read Rmeich

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

image.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.

items.title: The 'cheese grater' bowls that fed a generation of skaters

items.title: Watchdog seeks upgrade to 'harmful' youth cells to improve access to justice

children.content: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

items.title: Award-winning artist on how she paints in miniature

children.content: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.
